name: Grilled Chicken/Fish with Cucumber-Cilantro Beurre Blanc
recipeIngredient: 1 or 2 serrano or jalapeno chiles, coarsely chopped
4 whole chicken breasts or 4 fish fillets
1/2 medium cucumber, peeled, halved and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the oil, chiles, chopped cilantro, and salt. Marinate the chicken or fish in this mixture for 1 1/2 hours.
2. Grill the chicken or fish until barely done.
3. While the chicken is cooking, melt 3 tablespoons of butter in a skillet and cook the cucumber until softened, about 3 minutes.
4. In a small saucepan, boil the white wine and shallots together until reduced to 1 tablespoon.
5. Remove the saucepan from the heat and add the remaining butter to the saucepan bit by bit, whisking constantly.
6. Add the softened cucumber and season with lime juice, cilantro, salt, and pepper to taste.
7. Serve the beurre blanc over the chicken or fish.
Notes: With a little practice, you should be able to make the sauce in the time it takes for the chicken or fish to cook. If you start the sauce ahead of time, keep it warm in a water bath, or reheat it very gently if at all. This is one of the recipes from the first Cafe Terra Cotta menu. The sauce can be served on chicken or fish as described here, or with pork or veal. The cucumbers add not only a subtle herbaceous taste but also a crunchy texture and a beautiful shade of green as they glisten in the buttery sauce.
